description: Before the shepherds take their flocks of sheep to the pasture, there are 100 hectares of grass available.
Given the current situation, if each shepherd take more than 10 flocks of sheep to the pasture, consuming 10 hectares of grass, the available grass in the pasture will decrease next month
Kate took 10 flocks of sheep to the pasture, thus consuming 10 hectares of grass.
The mayor and the community members agreed to each maintain 10 flocks per month to ensure the pasture remains healthy and sustainable.
conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly pasture report. John consumed 10 hectares of grass. Kate consumed 10 hectares of grass. Jack consumed 10 hectares of grass. Emma consumed 10 hectares of grass. Luke consumed 10 hectares of grass.
John

It looks like we all took the same amount of sheep this month, which is good for the pasture's health. If we keep this up, we can ensure the grass remains sustainable. Does everyone agree to stick to 10 flocks each next month as well?
Kate

I agree, John. Sticking to 10 flocks each seems like a sustainable plan. It ensures that the grass can regenerate fully and we can continue to benefit in the long run. Does anyone have any concerns or suggestions?
Jack

I agree with John and Kate. Keeping our flocks to 10 each month ensures the pasture remains healthy and sustainable. This way, we can all continue to benefit without risking the depletion of the grass. Let's stick to this plan.
Emma

I agree with everyone. Sticking to 10 flocks each month is a smart strategy to ensure the pasture remains healthy and sustainable. Let's all commit to this plan and keep the pasture thriving for the long term.
